title: scPerturb Harmonized Single-Cell Perturbation Resource

data_urls.access_notes: Paper describes harmonized data access and original source datasets.

instructions: Use the scPerturb website or Zenodo records to select one harmonized h5ad dataset first.
Record original dataset, modality, cell type, perturbation, target gene, dose/time if present, QC fields, and split.
Expand to cross-dataset audits only after validating that perturbation labels and gene identifiers are harmonized.

scale.download_size: 44 harmonized public single-cell perturbation datasets; individual h5ad files vary substantially

recommended_axes.name: perturbation_target_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Gene, pathway, GO, protein-interaction, or embedding similarity between perturbation targets.

recommended_axes.name: cell_type_and_dataset_support
recommended_axes.definition: Cell type, donor, technology, lab, or original-dataset similarity.

recommended_axes.name: response_effect_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of perturbation effect vectors or E-distance-style response distances.

recommended_axes.name: modality_support
recommended_axes.definition: Support across RNA, ATAC, protein, or multiomic readouts.

useful_for: Providing a broad public perturbation resource for SPECTRA Dataset Scout.
Testing whether perturbation-response models generalize across target genes, cell types, technologies, and datasets.
Letting SPECTRA agents pull a new dataset when a local benchmark lacks biological metadata.
leakage_risks: The same perturbation target can appear in multiple datasets or cell types.
Dataset/lab/batch labels can dominate biological signal.
Response-effect similarity is post-hoc unless computed only on training labels or external biology.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip surfaced scPerturb as a harmonized perturbation data resource; official website and Nature Methods paper confirm 44 public perturbation-response datasets and Zenodo access.
